channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mercedes Demonstrates Pre-Season Pace Advantage in Melbourne, Validating Verstappen's Concerns
item.description: After weeks of speculation over 'sandbagging' in Formula One, Mercedes confirmed their strength by securing a front-row lockout in Melbourne, proving they were indeed ahead of the pack. Max Verstappen...

item.title: Small Explosion Occurs at US Embassy in Oslo, No Injuries Reported
item.description: Early Sunday morning, the US embassy in Oslo experienced a small explosion at its public entrance. Police in Norway said large resources were sent to the area around 01:00 local time to respond. Fortu...

item.title: Developments in US-Iran Conflict: Strikes, Leadership Change in Iran, and International Reactions
item.description: The conflict between the United States and Iran has escalated following the death of Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, at the start of hostilities. US and Israeli forces have conducted st...

item.title: Over 50,000 Indians Return from Middle East After Airspace Reopens Amid Conflict
item.description: Following the reopening of airspace in the Middle East, a total of 52,000 Indian citizens have returned safely to India. This mass repatriation comes amid heightened tensions in the region due to the ...

item.title: Opposition Leaders Kalonzo and Gachagua Demand Investigation into Passport Irregularities Linked to Russian Recruitment
item.description: Opposition leaders Kalonzo Musyoka and Rigathi Gachagua have teamed up to demand a thorough probe into allegations of Kenyan passports being sold to facilitate recruitment for Russia's war in Ukraine....

item.title: Corey Perry returns to Tampa Bay Lightning aiming for second Stanley Cup
item.description: Corey Perry has returned to the Tampa Bay Lightning after being traded from the Los Angeles Kings, with the aim of adding a second Stanley Cup to his career achievements. The 40-year-old veteran forwa...

item.title: Uttarakhand Governor's Helicopter Lands Safely After Technical Issue
item.description: On Sunday, the helicopter carrying Uttarakhand Governor, Lieutenant General Gurmit Singh (retd), had to make an emergency landing at the GVK helipad in Srinagar town of Pauri Garhwal due to certain te...

item.title: Quezon City gas stations experience low fuel supply ahead of scheduled oil price increase
item.description: Several gas stations in Quezon City are experiencing reduced fuel availability as motorists rush to fill up their tanks ahead of the scheduled oil price hike. The Department of Energy has confirmed th...

item.title: Conflict Escalates in Middle East, Targeting Energy and Water Infrastructure
item.description: On the ninth day of the ongoing conflict involving the US, Israel, and Iran, the war is now extending beyond missiles and drones to critical economic and civilian infrastructure. Efforts at diplomatic...

item.title: Surfing and Tourism in Siargao: How Waves Shape Community and Family Life
item.description: Surfing in Siargao has become a central example of how sports tourism can positively impact local communities. The island’s waves not only attract international competitions organized by the World Sur...

item.title: OpenAI executive resigns over Pentagon AI partnership concerns
item.description: Caitlin Kalinowski, who was leading hardware operations at OpenAI, announced her resignation on Saturday due to ethical and governance concerns regarding the company's recent agreement with the United...

item.title: Federal-State Conflicts in U.S. Federalism: Immigration Enforcement and Historical Precedents
item.description: The article examines ongoing tensions between the federal government and U.S. states, particularly amid the Trump administration's aggressive immigration policies. Democratic-led states are refusing t...

item.title: Tehran Reduces Motorists' Fuel Allowance Following Israeli Strikes on Oil Storage
item.description: Authorities in Tehran have implemented fuel rationing for motorists after Israeli airstrikes damaged oil storage facilities in the Iranian capital and nearby Alborz province. The Tehran governor annou...

item.title: Ikechi Odigbo Launches Reflective Book on Life and Destiny at 60
item.description: Ikechi Odigbo, wey be well-known advertising executive and former CEO of DDB Lagos, don launch him new book titled Reality Through the Lens of Eternity. The book dey explore deep meaning of life, love...

item.title: South Africa’s Trade Partnerships with the Middle East Face Geopolitical Challenges
item.description: South Africa has been actively working to diversify its trade and investment partners, increasingly looking towards Middle Eastern economies. These partnerships aim to help the country move up the min...

item.title: Exploring the Benefits and Drawbacks of Salary Sacrifice to Reduce Tax Bands
item.description: Sky News' Money newsletter, which reaches over 170,000 subscribers, provides weekly insights and guidance on personal finance topics. This upcoming Friday, the newsletter will examine the advantages a...

item.title: History of Carter Bridge: Lagos' 125-Year-Old Colonial Link Set for Demolition and Reconstruction
item.description: Carter Bridge, one of Lagos' oldest structures, was first built in 1901 during British colonial rule under Governor Sir Gilbert Thomas Carter, after whom it was named. Back then, Lagos Island was the ...

item.title: Tanzania fights new drug threat in food products
item.description: Tanzania is facing a serious challenge in its fight against drugs. Criminals are becoming more dangerous, now even putting drugs in biscuits and other foods, aiming to secretly trap young people and u...

item.title: Janelle Dodd tackles period poverty through community-led initiatives
item.description: Janelle Dodd, a crafter from Sydney, has spent nearly a decade leading the Ryde team of Days for Girls, an international not-for-profit focused on addressing period poverty. The organisation creates m...

item.title: Brumbies face scheduling challenges due to Wallabies player rest protocols
item.description: The ACT Brumbies are navigating a tricky start to their Super Rugby Pacific season, compounded by Rugby Australia's player management protocols. Head coach Stephen Larkham faces a scheduling challenge...
